mnemonics: Modelers Make Everything Visible On Screens
keyPoints: Flow-oriented modeling involves designing the flow of the system from the outside in
Identify the main activities and processes that occur within the system
Focus on the interactions between systems, users, and external entities
Consider the data flows and the relationships between different data entities
Use visual representations to create a clear and concise model
Break down complex systems into smaller, more manageable components
Elaborate on each component to create a detailed and accurate model
